events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need parse image: Evacuation decay test pump isolated. Microns @ isolation t=0 = 457. Microns @ t=10 min = 549. So pressure rose 92 microns in 10 min. Need diagnose. In HVAC-R evacuation, standing decay test: isolate pump, watch micron gauge. Acceptable rise typically <500 microns over time? Many standards: system should hold below 500 microns and not rise more than 500 microns in 10 min? Actually common: final vacuum 500 microns or less, decay test no more than 500 microns rise over 10-30 min? Let's recall. Many manufacturers: evacuate to 500 microns, isolate pump, if pressure rises above 1000 microns in 10 minutes indicates moisture or leak. Some say rise less than 500 microns in 30 min. Here t0 457, t10 549, rise 92 microns. That's very small. So pass. But wait: initial 457 microns is above 500? Actually 457 < 500, good. Final 549 >500 but rise small. Some criteria require final below 500? Decay test often measures rise, not absolute after 10 min. If initial below 500 and rise only 92 microns, indicates no significant leak/outgassing. So pass, low risk. Need output JSON with fields. value = 549 (10-minute reading). rate = rise per minute? (549-457)/10 = 9.2 microns/min. event_time 10.0. work_stage tested/inspected. percent_complete maybe 90. findings empty? Could include "Vacuum decay within acceptable limits". actions maybe "Proceed with charging/system startup". references EPA 608, mfr specs. component_conditions empty. decision pass. risk low. confidence high 0.95. Need ensure valid JSON.

  ],
  "rationale": "The system was evacuated to 457 microns and isolated. After 10 minutes the micron gauge rose only 92 microns to 549 microns, indicating minimal outgassing or leakage and an acceptable standing decay test result.",

    "EPA 608",
    "OEM evacuation and decay specifications"
  ]
}
